market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

title_variants: Spacious 3‑Bedroom Home for 4 Guests – Top Rating
analysis.rewritten_desc: Experience upscale city living in this 3‑bedroom luxury apartment, offered as an entire home for up to four guests. The space has earned a strong 4.8‑star rating from five recent reviewers, reflecting the care taken to provide a comfortable stay. At a nightly rate of £200.04, the apartment positions itself at the higher end of the market, which is 14 % above the city’s median nightly price of £175. Over the next 90 days the calendar shows 18.5 % occupancy, leaving 73 open nights that could be filled with the right adjustments. Host Yasir is new to Airbnb, bringing fresh energy to the listing. While the property does not yet highlight any specific amenities, the modern design and generous layout make it ideal for families or small groups seeking a stylish base. With a focus on delivering a premium experience, the apartment is ready to attract discerning travelers looking for a high‑quality, well‑rated stay in the city.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a bright, high‑resolution image of the main living area with any city view as the cover photo; follow with dedicated photos of each bedroom, kitchen, and bathroom to showcase the full space.
key_diagnosis: Rate adjustment: Your nightly price of £200.04 is 14 % above the cohort median (£175) while occupancy is only 18.5 % versus the market median of 28.3 %; lowering the rate to around £170 could close the gap and boost bookings.
Add high‑value amenities: Introducing outdoor space, free parking, and entertainment features will align the listing with top‑value categories and justify the premium price.

Photo upgrade: The current photo count is unknown, likely insufficient; add clear shots of the living room, each bedroom, kitchen, and bathroom, and set the living‑room view as the lead image to improve visual appeal.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your listing charges £200/night – 14 % above the city median – yet only 18.5 % of the next 90 days are booked.
